items.rationale: His signature thesis: crypto as the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies, financing real-world assets and making 'atoms look more like bits.' Now a core mandate as GP.
items.age_note: Consistently restated across 14+ months (Jan 2025 → Jan 2026), most recently 2026-01-29; the most durable and intense theme in the corpus.

citations.quote: DeFi's next step is into the real world by financing physical infrastructure. The future of crypto is real (assets).
I'll focus on investing in DeFi, DePIN, and the infrastructure powering those applications to bring finance and physical infrastructure onchain.
DePIN is eating the world

items.rationale: Named explicit GP focus area; sees capital as one of three networks crypto supercharges. Actively researching perps cohort retention and 'invading the order books onchain.'
items.age_note: Reaffirmed at GP announcement (2025-11-10) and into 2026; active perps/order-book interest Jan 2026.

citations.quote: I'm most excited about capital, information, and energy as the networks that crypto can supercharge.
We're invading the order books onchain
We're thrilled to partner with Jito and support their mission to build Internet Capital Markets on Solana!

items.rationale: Believes privacy is now a key product bottleneck given institutional adoption and agent economies; backs client-side proving (Miden), collaborative SNARKs (Taceo), and Seismic.
items.age_note: Strong through 2025 (Miden Apr, Taceo Jul, Seismic Nov 2025); evolved from 2024 ZK/coSNARK threads into an explicit privacy-as-bottleneck conviction.

citations.quote: Privacy is essential... This all sets up privacy as a key bottleneck and important product problem that wasn't the case years ago.
Client-side proving also offers flexible privacy that public chains today don't.
Get ready for a Seismic impact on the privacy landscape

items.rationale: Sees institutions moving onchain as the dominant 2025-2026 shift, with Wall Street adopting blockchains for ledger efficiency (not ideology). His most recent statement in the corpus.
items.age_note: Most recent view (2026-03-30) reinforces 2025-08-12 thesis; current and strengthening.

citations.quote: Wall Street is adopting blockchains as a schelling point to upgrade their atrophying core ledgers.
I believe 2025 is the year institutions move onchain.

items.rationale: Excited by open marketplaces for AI inference (Ambient's useful proof-of-work L1) and user-owned/operated AI companions as the best verifiable-AI use case; ties to 'information' network thesis.
items.age_note: Mid-2025 cluster (PIN AI 2024-11, verifiable AI Jun 2025, Ambient Jun 2025); he explicitly reversed prior skepticism of useful proof-of-work.

citations.quote: I had personally been very skeptical of useful proof of work... but the Ambient team re-ignited my enthusiasm for the idea almost immediately.
What's the best use case for verifiable AI? I believe it's user owned and operated companions.

items.rationale: Energy is one of his three named supercharge-able networks; backs distributed energy (Daylight) and floats decentralized water/energy-futures markets.
items.age_note: Part of Nov 2025 GP thesis; Daylight support Oct 2025; consistent subset of DePIN.

citations.quote: support Daylight on their mission to revitalize the American energy grid and build a distributed energy network for the future.
capital, information, and energy as the networks that crypto can supercharge

items.rationale: Sees all-time-high stablecoin volume as a structural win and expects agent-driven micropayments to revive pay-per-view content models.
items.age_note: Mid-2025 (micropayments Jun, stablecoin volume Apr, compliant payments panel Oct 2025); not restated in 2026 but consistent.

citations.quote: Micropayments will make a comeback... Agents don't care about these payment decisions, and I believe more content will be paid per view.
all time high stablecoin volume (higher than visa or mastercard)

items.rationale: Believes proving networks become essential infrastructure and real-time proofs can unify fragmented Ethereum rollups; backed Fermah, T1, Syndicate.
items.age_note: Heavily 2024 (Fermah/T1 Oct-Nov 2024); lighter touch since (NEAR intents Nov 2025). Older conviction, not strongly restated recently — weight reduced.

citations.quote: I believe proving networks will become essential for blockchains
Seamless interoperability is my holy grail for rollups, and T1 is working to finally make it possible.

items.rationale: Worries unified liquidity may not arrive before vertically integrated L1s bootstrap enough liquidity, threatening Ethereum's rollup-centric model.
items.age_note: Stated 2024-10/2024-11; not restated in 2025-2026. Treat as an older, possibly softening concern — his RTP/interop bets are the proposed remedy.

citations.quote: fragmented liquidity and fractured state present a challenge and an opportunity.
Can Ethereum rollups ship seamless interoperability to stop liquidity fragmentation before the vertically integrated L1s bootstrap enough liquidity?

items.rationale: Concerned that 'decentralization' and 'DePIN' are misused buzzwords that lose meaning, and that projects selling 'vibes' rather than value are really religion/entertainment.
items.age_note: Sustained 2025 stance (DePIN dilution Jan, decentralization-not-a-goal Jul, vibes-not-value Jun 2025); a framing/discipline concern more than a market-risk concern.

citations.quote: I think we should stop using the word decentralization. Decentralization is not a goal
If the product is vibes and not value, find a different product. Or realize you're selling religion and entertainment

items.rationale: Sees insurance as an obviously valuable but historically failed crypto category — premiums too high, capital rehypothecation inefficient — though he thinks conditions are improving.
items.age_note: Raised Jan 2025 and again May 2025 ('most compelling crypto insurance protocols?') — an open problem he's watching, not a thesis-breaker.

citations.quote: Insurance seems like an obviously good idea for crypto. Why has it never worked? Perhaps because the premiums would be too high
What are the most compelling crypto insurance protocols?

items.rationale: Praises Solana's pragmatic, shipping-over-maximalism culture and application-controlled execution; views it as the venue for the world's most liquid financial markets (Jito, Ambient fork).
items.age_note: Mid-to-late 2025 (ACE/pragmatism Jul, Jito Oct, Ambient forking Solana Jun 2025); consistent and recent.

citations.quote: There are no zealots in Solana, only pragmatic engineers who want to build a platform that can support the world's most liquid financial markets.
shipping beats maximalism

items.rationale: Notes growing intents volume and praises the NEAR–Zashi integration UX, fitting his interoperability and seamless-UX priorities.
items.age_note: Single recent positive mention (2025-11-03); not restated — observation rather than deep conviction.

citations.quote: NEARProtocol intents volume is growing. Their integration with zashi_app also makes for a really nice UX in my opinion

risk_regime.rationale: Consistently and emphatically optimistic: dismisses cynicism as self-defeating, cites regulatory wins, institutional adoption, and record stablecoin volume as tailwinds, and frames 2025+ as the 'sales era' where crypto delivers real value. His most recent statements (Wall Street adoption, 'invading order books') are bullish on capital flowing onchain.

notes: Guy's view is highly coherent and durable, organized around three networks he named at his Nov-2025 GP promotion: capital (DeFi), information (decentralized/verifiable AI), and energy/physical infrastructure (DePIN). DePIN is his single strongest and most-restated conviction; privacy/client-side proving is a clear secondary thesis that evolved out of his 2024 ZK/coSNARK work. Notable reversals: he abandoned skepticism of useful proof-of-work after meeting Ambient (2025-06-25), and his 2024 'proving networks/Ethereum interop' enthusiasm has faded relative to DePIN/DeFi/privacy (downgraded to persisted). He explicitly rejects 'decentralization' as a goal, preferring concrete goals (censorship resistance, composability, eliminating platform risk) — a framing concern, not a bearish signal. He names few liquid tokens; most picks are early-stage/equity (CSX accelerator) so token-level read is thin. He is an avowed optimist ('weaponized optimism,' 'hopepunk'); a large fraction of the corpus is non-market personal/cultural commentary, filtered out here. Where statements conflicted, most-recent (2026) views were weighted; no hard reversals on the major sector theses.
